summarization.a: The figure illustrates how resistivity decreases sharply with increasing H₂ plasma exposure time. Both spectroscopic ellipsometry (triangles) and 4-point probe (circles) measurements show consistent results, with the resistivity dropping rapidly in the first few seconds before stabilizing at longer exposure times. The data demonstrate that extended H₂ plasma treatment improves conductivity by reducing film defects.

| H₂ Plasma Exposure Time (s) | Resistivity (µΩ·cm) | Measurement Method         |
|-----------------------------|---------------------|----------------------------|
| 0                           | 10000               | 4-point probe              |
| 1                           | 1000                | 4-point probe              |
| 5                           | 300                 | Spectroscopic ellipsometry |
| 10                          | 200                 | Spectroscopic ellipsometry |
| 20                          | 120                 | Spectroscopic ellipsometry |
| 30                          | 100                 | Spectroscopic ellipsometry |

a.question: What property of the film is being measured?
How does resistivity vary with increasing H₂ plasma exposure time?
a.answer: Resistivity decreases rapidly with increasing plasma exposure time, dropping several orders of magnitude within the first few seconds and then stabilizing, indicating improved conductivity due to surface modification.

a.question: Which techniques were used to measure resistivity?
Does increasing H₂ plasma exposure time enhance the film’s conductivity
